heroSubtitle: Analyzing Systems · Informing Policy · Driving Impact
aboutHeadline: Quantitative analyst bridging applied mathematics, policy analysis, and operations leadership.
aboutParagraphs: I am a quantitative analyst with training in applied mathematics and political science from UCLA. My work focuses on forecasting under uncertainty, building scalable operational systems, and translating complex data into strategic insights for diverse stakeholders. I have recently been experimenting extensively with AI agents like Gemini 3.5 Flash and Claude Opus 4.6 in Google Antigravity to build new games and tools to share with the world.

With experience spanning climate consulting at Clark Street Associates, NGO alliance development in Argentina, and large-scale program operations at UCLA, I combine rigorous mathematical modeling with policy analysis. I am passionate about leveraging  data systems to solve today's challenges, and believe that AI is the power tool to do so quickly and efficiently.

In my spare time, besides developing new tools to share, I also care for my dog Libby and have recently done several car repairs (engine drive belt replacement, gasket cover replacement, + more) with Dad in the driveway. For sport, I joined Quadball in my third year at UCLA, and I am continuing to play now with the Silicon Valley Vipers!
metaSkills: Antigravity/Codex, Python, R, SQL, Excel/Sheets, C++, JavaScript, Spanish
galleryTravelDescription: Visiting natural landscapes and the Wonders of the World!
galleryVolunteeringDescription: Providing educational outreach and academic mentoring to those less fortunate than I.
